description: Unicorn is a lightweight multi-platform, multi-architecture CPU emulator framework, based on QEMU. Useful for binary emulation in exploits.

documentation.example: from unicorn import Uc, UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64; u = Uc(UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64); u.mem_map(0x1000, 0x1000)
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/unicorn && python3 unicorn.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/unicorn && python3 unicorn.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/unicorn && python3 unicorn.py --help`. CPU emulator engine (multi-arch).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: CPU emulator engine (multi-arch).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: CPU emulator engine (multi-arch).

metadata.example: from unicorn import Uc, UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64; u = Uc(UC_ARCH_X86, UC_MODE_64); u.mem_map(0x1000, 0x1000)

name: unicorn

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
